hypothesis: Ch2 citation Goodhart: citation count rewards presence not mechanism invocation
method: citation_mechanism.py: classify L-NNN references as INVOKE (per/apply/enforce patterns) or MENTION (found/measured/bare refs). Compute invoke_ratio per lesson.
verdict: CONFIRMED — Ch2 Goodhart is real. 3.6% aggregate invoke rate across top-20 cited lessons. 100% have invoke_ratio < 10%. Even L-601 (highest cited, 930 in-degree) converts at only 6.6%. Citations overwhelmingly reward presence, not mechanism invocation.
falsification_target: Citation count is a valid quality signal for lesson mechanism use
falsification_outcome: FALSIFIED — r(in_degree, invoke_ratio)=0.467 moderate but absolute invoke rates (0-6.6%) are below actionable threshold. Citation count is dominated by mention noise.
fix_applied: reward_theory.py Ch2 now uses citation_mechanism.py invoke_ratio instead of raw in-degree count. Ch2 status: MISALIGNED with quantified measurement.
successor: Build structural pressure toward mechanism invocation (L-601 pattern): enforce that Cites: header entries must include invocation evidence
